rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK FKA GREENWOOD TRUST COMPANY, VS. MARIA R CLEMENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Discover Bank Fka Greenwood Trust Company'S Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012, Line 14, Discover Bank Fka Greenwood Trust Company'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Granted. The levying officer shall withhold $ 150.00 per pay period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KARIM H. TAHIR VS. JIMMY BUDIHALIM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Appoint Receiver Fro 1607 Milvia Street And 2321 Blake St, In Berkeley, California
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT JIMMY BUDIHALIM's Motion To Appoint Receiver For 1607 Milvia Street And 2321 Blake St, In Berkeley, California. The Court continues this motion to December 19, 2012. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the sale of the Blake Street and Milvia Street properties. Plaintiffs shall file a supplemental brief regarding the status of the properties by December 7, 2012. Defendants to file a supplemental brief by December 13, 2012. In addition, Defendants shall nominate a receiver and present evidence regarding his/her qualifications to act as receiver in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The plaintiffs are required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of their counsel in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID LO et al VS. DANIEL S. LEE AKA DAN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Answers To Interrogatories And Requests For Admission, For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees, Costs And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFFS DAVID LO, DANIEL LO, ALICE LO'S Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Answers To Interrogatories And Requests For Admission, For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees, Costs And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Prior to hearing, parties are to meet and confer regarding what further responsive information Defendant, Daniel Lo, has available to provide in response to Interrogatory No. 17.1. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO PLASCENCIA VS. ALL SEAS WHOLESALE INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Relating To Deft'S Failure To Comply With Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 1, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3, PLAINTIFF ALFREDO PLASCENCIA'S (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Relating To Defendant's Failure To Comply With Court Order Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Court requests further information concerning failure to produce all Plaintiff's time cards for shifts prior to July, 2012, and information concerning minimum time necessary to complete full production of requested documents. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP INC. VS. GRO-POWER INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT GRO-POWER INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff has adequately pled a violation of Prop. 65, and the pleadings are not uncertain. Plaintiff has alleged the specific product, Gro-Power Flower 'n' Bloom® 3-12-12, 8.8 lbs., that contains Cadmium and Cadmium Compounds. rulings.Case Title: GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION VS. BRANDO EMPIRE SAN FRANCISCO, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT BRANDO EMPIRE SAN FRANCISCO, LLC, BRANDY HO, JIMMY LAM's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. For the first and sixth causes of action, the nature of each contract may be ascertained by the allegations.
